Suing for a refund of $124,000 due to a breach of contract of a contractor who promised to provide "gut to studs"-type renovation services for a 1,730 sq. ft., 2-story home. The contracts we signed, and have paid for, total $179,000, but the work is only 20% finished after 13 months of continual delays. Contractor refused to attend mediation; his attorney promised another "work schedule" on 9-17-24, due by 9-27-24, but no information has been received as of this writing. We do not want this contractor to proceed because he is a crook, and we need our money refunded so we can continue with the project. The contractor has refused to do so and insists on going forward with yet another "work schedule". The Oregon Construction Contractors Board letter dated 9-18-24 states that we must file a court action within 30 days of their letter, or their file of our complaint will be closed. Please advise our next steps. Thanks you
